subject: test(daisyui): add three-layer automated regression coverage
body:
Adds the complete test infrastructure for the DaisyUI v5 component-class
migration. Three complementary layers:

Layer 1: Source-level tripwire (scripts/__tests__/daisyui-surfaces.test.mjs)

  30 assertions running via node:test on every `npm test` invocation.
  ~230ms runtime, no browser, no transpilation. Catches:

  - Per-phase DaisyUI class names at expected call sites (Phase 1-10).
  - Removed custom classes (quick-guide-*, .toast-*, filter-select,
    stat-card, metric-selector, pin-btn, etc.) must not be
    re-introduced.
  - v4 cruft regression guard for -bordered / -compact / -bordered.
  - Dead-marker-class sweep across dashboard/js/**/*.jsx that flags
    lines with className attributes referencing removed markers.
  - Hardcoded Tailwind color shade sweep that flags bg-green-500 /
    bg-red-500 etc. in JSX (must use DaisyUI semantic tokens).
  - Built CSS verification: checks dist/assets/index-*.css ships
    every DaisyUI component class we reference (.modal, .btn,
    .card, .badge, .alert, .toast, .tabs, .select, .input,
    .checkbox, .join, .loading, .progress + their variants). Skips
    gracefully when dist/ is absent for fresh-clone test runs.
  - The 8 semantic bg-* tokens (success/info/warning/error/primary/
    secondary/accent/neutral) ship in the built CSS.

  Comment-stripping helper lets rationale blocks in the source
  intentionally reference removed class names without false-triggering
  the regression checks. Handles {/* ... */} JSX comments and /* ... */
  JS block comments plus line-level // comments.

Layer 2: Runtime smoke (dashboard/e2e/daisyui-surfaces.spec.js)

  14 Playwright tests walking the TESTING_GUIDE "DaisyUI component-class
  migration" checklist in a real Chromium instance. Catches regressions
  that slip past the source-level tripwire — conditional classNames
  that only fire in specific React state, portal / stacking-context
  bugs, Chart.js theme tracking, interaction flows.

  Key tests:
  - QuickGuide modal open/close (Phase 1).
  - Toast runtime class shape (Phase 2).
  - CollapsibleSection + ProjectCard DaisyUI card structure (Phase 5).
  - Header icon button classes (Phase 6).
  - FilterSidebar Include/Exclude join segmented (Phase 6).
  - TabBar tabs + tab-active composition with ARIA (Phase 7).
  - SettingsPane select / FilterSidebar date inputs (Phase 8) —
    explicit toHaveClass(/select-bordered/).not() regression guard
    for the v4 cruft bug.
  - HamburgerMenu portal (Phase 9) — walks the DOM parent chain
    via page.evaluate() to verify the dropdown is parented to
    document.body, not .dashboard-header. Reads
    getComputedStyle(nav).position to assert "fixed" confirming
    createPortal worked and the CSS override landed.
  - FilterSidebar multi-select checkbox classes (Phase 10).
  - Loading spinner: all DaisyUI `loading loading-spinner`, zero
    shadow-class re-introductions.
  - Progress bars: native <progress> with DaisyUI progress-variant.
  - Chart dataset backgroundColor changes when theme changes —
    reads dataset[0].backgroundColor before + after a theme swap.

Layer 3: Visual regression (dashboard/e2e/visual/theme-baselines.spec.js)

  48 screenshot baselines = 6 tabs × (4 light + 4 dark) themes.
  Captured at 1280x720 (matches TESTING_GUIDE manual viewport),
  full-page so long scrolling sections are covered.

  Theme switch via localStorage + reload — same path users hit on
  fresh load, exercises the flash-prevention script + AppContext
  bootstrap. Sanity-checks html[data-theme] before each capture.

  maxDiffPixelRatio: 0.002 (0.2%) tolerates font-rendering variance
  across headless Chromium versions without masking real regressions.

  Purpose: catch the class of bug the select-bordered v4 cruft fell
  into — visually correct in the default theme, broken in non-default
  themes, invisible to class-name assertions.

Playwright config (playwright.config.js):

- Two projects: `smoke` (DOM assertions) + `visual` (screenshot
  baselines). Smoke runs by default; visual runs explicitly.
- webServer runs `npm run build && npm run preview --strictPort`
  so tests always hit the production build.
- launchOptions honors PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_EXECUTABLE_PATH so
  sandboxed / firewalled environments can point at a system
  Chromium when Playwright's managed CDN is blocked.
- CI-aware: forbidOnly rejects test.only() on CI, retries=2 on
  CI vs 0 locally, dot reporter on CI vs HTML locally.
- reuseExistingServer=true locally so iteration is fast.

package.json scripts:

- test:e2e — runs smoke project
- test:e2e:install — installs Chromium + OS deps
- test:e2e:ui — Playwright interactive debug UI
- test:visual — runs visual regression project
- test:visual:update — regenerates screenshot baselines after
  intentional visual changes

dashboard/e2e/README.md:

- Three-tier coverage overview table.
- Per-spec rationale (what each file catches and why).
- Standard Chromium install path + sandboxed/system-browser
  fallback recipe.
- GitHub Actions CI recipe with `playwright install --with-deps`.
- Troubleshooting section covering timeout errors, tiny pixel
  diffs on fresh machines, "Host not allowed" errors on sandboxed
  networks.

.gitignore:

- /test-results/, /playwright-report/, /playwright/.cache/ for
  Playwright transient output.
- **/*-actual.png and **/*-diff.png for visual-diff PNGs produced
  on failure.
- Screenshot BASELINES under dashboard/e2e/visual/__screenshots__/
  are deliberately NOT ignored — they're committed so CI diffs
  against them.

Verified:
- `./node_modules/.bin/vite build` clean.
- `npm test` → 51/51 pass (21 existing oklchToHex + 30 new surface
  assertions).
- `playwright test --list` enumerates all 62 tests (14 smoke + 48
  visual) across both projects — config parses, specs are
  discoverable. Tests can't run in this sandbox (no Chromium binary
  available, CDN blocked) but are ready for CI.

Tags: testing, automation, daisyui, migration, playwright, qa
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 2
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-component-migration
Semver: minor
